page.title: Personal Nutrition Workbench
page.description: Recipe library + food classification + nutrition facts + daily plans + meal logs

overview.heroTitle: Today doesn't need to be perfect. It just needs to care for you.
overview.heroDescription: Bring recipes, foods, nutrition, and real meals into one page so you can see what to eat, what's missing, and what to do next.
overview.arrangedSlotsHint: Empty slots are not pressure, only planning signals
overview.foodsHint: The food library is the base layer for recipes and nutrition
overview.recipesHint: Only what you can make or want to recreate
overview.todayPlateDesc: Shape the plan into an executable day by meal slot.
overview.emptySlot: Not arranged yet, and that's allowed
overview.nutritionPulseDesc: A light estimate that never treats missing data as zero.
overview.completionCopy: There is already a plan today. The remaining work is making it easy to execute.
overview.profileDesc: Read the boundaries and current goal before arranging today's executable meals.

profileEdit.title: Edit Dietary Profile
profileEdit.description: The dietary profile records the current phase goal, hard boundaries, and soft stances that should shape daily plans.
profileEdit.intentNotePlaceholder: For example: observe the relationship between delivery food, caffeine, and sleep.
profileEdit.constraintsDescription: Hard constraints cover allergies, medical limits, or religious and cultural boundaries. Plans and suggestions should avoid them first.
profileEdit.constraintLabelPlaceholder: For example: reduce caffeine at night
profileEdit.stancesDescription: Soft stances are not bans. They are directions this phase should care about a little more.
profileEdit.stanceNotePlaceholder: How should it shape the plan?

validation.labelRequired: Complete the constraint or stance labels

dailyPlan.title: Daily Plan
dailyPlan.planNote: A plan is a fallback, not a check-in pressure
dailyPlan.emptySlot: Leave this meal blank for now
dailyPlan.emptySlotsHint: They can stay blank, or become a low-friction fallback plan
dailyPlan.pendingNutritionHint: Missing data is never counted as zero
dailyPlan.replacementHint: Suggested from unplanned recipes with more complete nutrition data.
dailyPlan.noReplacement: No replacement recipes yet. Add recipes or complete nutrition data first.
dailyPlan.missingNutrition: {{count}} entries have pending nutrition data and were not counted as zero.

dailyPlanEdit.description: Daily plans can reference recipes, foods, or temporary text. Empty meal slots can stay blank.
dailyPlanEdit.slotsHint: Each meal slot can stay empty or combine multiple planned entries.
dailyPlanEdit.deleteBlocked: This plan has {{count}} linked meal logs and cannot be deleted yet.

validation.slotRequired: Keep at least one meal slot

recipes.title: Recipes
recipes.search: Search recipes, tags, or meal slots
recipes.recentFeedbackNoNote: This log has no extra note.
recipes.noRecentFeedback: No real meal feedback yet. It will appear here after you generate a log from a plan or add one manually.

recipeEdit.description: Recipes reference foods from the food library, and nutrition is derived from ingredients.
recipeEdit.name: Recipe Name
recipeEdit.summaryPlaceholder: What situation is this recipe good for?
recipeEdit.missingFoodMemory: Linked food memory no longer exists
recipeEdit.ingredientsHint: Ingredients come from the food library; nutrition is derived by amount.
recipeEdit.deleteBlocked: This recipe is referenced by {{count}} plans or logs and cannot be deleted yet.

validation.mealRoleRequired: Select at least one meal role
validation.ingredientsRequired: Add at least one valid ingredient

foods.title: Food Classification
foods.allFoodsDesc: Browse and search the whole food library across categories.
foods.noNote: No note yet. Storage, usage, and constraints can be added later.
foods.noDietaryTags: No dietary tags or allergens yet.
foods.profileMissingCopy: This food can still be used in recipes and plans, but the nutrition table will show it as pending.

categoryEdit.description: Food categories organize the food library and shape list views, filters, and nutrition table reading paths.
categoryEdit.name: Category Name
categoryEdit.descriptionPlaceholder: How does this category help you choose foods?
categoryEdit.deleteBlocked: This category is referenced by {{count}} foods and cannot be deleted yet.

foodEdit.description: Foods are the base data source for recipes, daily plans, and nutrition facts.
foodEdit.name: Food Name
foodEdit.namePlaceholder: For example: egg, tomato, brown rice
foodEdit.nutritionDescription: Maintain values per 100g / 100ml. Blank fields are shown as pending, not counted as zero.
foodEdit.deleteBlocked: This food is referenced by {{count}} recipes, plans, or logs and cannot be deleted yet.

nutrients.title: Nutrition Facts
nutrients.emptyHint: Clear search, switch categories, or turn off the pending-only filter.
nutrients.drawerDescription: This detail view currently combines available nutrient fields with stable simulated scores; real targets can be connected later.
nutrients.chartHint: Like a game stat radar: farther out means stronger presence; missing fields are temporarily simulated.

logs.title: Meal Logs
logs.description: Record what actually happened so the plan can be calibrated.
logs.search: Search meals, scenes, feedback, or food memories
logs.boundary: This keeps the observation clues: why you ate, how it felt afterward, and why the plan changed. It calibrates plans, not judges you.

logEdit.description: Meal logs help you review what actually happened and can link back to a planned meal slot.
logEdit.missingPlannedSlot: Linked plan slot no longer exists
logEdit.missingFoodMemory: Linked food memory no longer exists
logEdit.entriesHint: Record recipes, individual foods, or temporary unstructured text.
logEdit.textTitlePlaceholder: For example: a delivery set meal
logEdit.changeReasonPlaceholder: If it differed from the plan, what happened?
logEdit.notePlaceholder: Body feeling, mood, context, or follow-up adjustment

validation.entryRequired: Add at least one meal entry
